glbopt1_state_map.note_blk7: blk7 is the BODY handler (routed-to by K1) AND a 2WAY that writes its own next-state K1, with succs {8,9} (its counter arms), NOT the dispatcher. It is a spurious writer and is filtered out of the plan.

back_edge: blk8 (body continue-arm, pred = blk7 BODY) redirected to blk7 -> a retreating edge to an ancestor = the reconstructed loop back-edge. blk6 (init) -> blk7 is the forward entry.
live_render: PASS via optblock_t (GLBOPT1, return change-count). Renders a clean do/while on the counter: init r+=0x11 outside; do { r^=0x22; sink=r; --counter } while(counter); then r-=0x33; sink; return. No dispatcher loop, no state constants, mba.verify() clean.
general_rule: The general insert-based unflatten -- redirect every GENUINE dispatcher-returning state-writer (succ == dispatcher head) to the handler its written state routes to (TERM-writers -> terminal) -- reconstructs a loop FOR FREE: a handler that writes a state routing back to an earlier handler becomes a real back-edge. This generalizes the linear P1 plan; L1 needs no loop-specific lowering op (PhaseCycleLowering unused).
dispatcher_head_detection: MODE (most common successor) of all state-writer blocks, NOT their intersection. A handler that is also a 2WAY (the loop body) contributes its arms, so an intersection collapses to empty (disp=-1). The mode is robust: dispatcher head blk2 appears 5x (from writers 1,6,8,9,10); the body's arms 8/9 appear once each.
spurious_writer_filter: Redirect only writers whose successor includes the dispatcher head. The body block (blk7) writes K1 but branches into its own arms -- redirecting 7 -> routing[K1]=7 would be a degenerate self-redirect. Filtering by succ==disp excludes it.
counter_induction_preserved: The counter (var_8? no -- counter is a separate slot; state_slot is %var_8.4) survives as the loop induction variable; it is never written a state constant, so _state_slot never confuses it for the state var. The do/while renders directly on it.
test: tests/system/runtime/hexrays/test_insert_unflatten_mini.py::TestInsertUnflattenLoop (test_dump_loop_structure + test_loop_renders_optblock); 10 passed / 1 xfailed across the lab file.
